req.title: Overdraft protection
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined.
preconditions: overdraft linked

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-012 specifies a two-tier debit decision: if checking balance is insufficient, attempt to draw from linked savings up to its available balance; if linked savings is also insufficient, decline the transaction. The test suite covers the happy path (overdraft covered by savings), the failure path (both accounts insufficient), and the critical boundary (exact balance match). Equivalence partitioning divides the state space into sufficient-checking, insufficient-checking-sufficient-savings, and insufficient-both. Boundary value analysis targets the exact balance thresholds. Negative path testing validates decline behavior. State transition coverage maps the account state transitions through overdraft logic.

tests.name: test_overdraft_draws_from_linked_savings_when_checking_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance = $500
Linked savings account exists with available balance = $1000
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee is $35
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $800 (exceeds checking by $300)
Verify request includes account_id, linked_account_id, amount, and overdraft_token
trace: R-FIN-012: overdraft linked → debit exceeds checking balance → linked savings debited up to available balance
R-FIN-012: expected outcomes → linked savings debited, fee assessed
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_draws_from_linked_savings_when_checking_insufficient():
    import requests
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    payload = {
        "account_id": "ACC001",
        "linked_account_id": "SAV001",
        "amount": 800,
        "overdraft_token": "valid_token_123"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["status"] == "approved"
    assert data["fee"] == 35
    assert data["savings_balance_after"] == 165
    assert "transaction_id" in data

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_both_accounts_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance = $200
Linked savings account exists with available balance = $300
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee = $35
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $600 (exceeds total available by $100)
Verify request includes valid account identifiers and overdraft token
trace: R-FIN-012: error pathways → declined when linked balance insufficient
R-FIN-012: debit exceeds checking balance → linked savings unavailable → transaction declined
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_declined_when_both_accounts_insufficient():
    import requests
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    payload = {
        "account_id": "ACC002",
        "linked_account_id": "SAV002",
        "amount": 600,
        "overdraft_token": "valid_token_456"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 402
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["status"] == "declined"
    assert data["reason"] == "insufficient_combined_balance"
    assert data.get("fee", 0) == 0
    assert "transaction_id" not in data

tests.name: test_overdraft_boundary_exact_linked_balance_available
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance = $100
Linked savings account exists with available balance = $50
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee = $35
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $150 (exactly checking + savings - fee)
Verify request structure and token validity
expected: Transaction recorded with final balance state
trace: R-FIN-012: boundary condition → debit amount equals checking + (linked - fee)
R-FIN-012: linked savings debited up to available balance (BVA: exact boundary)
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_boundary_exact_linked_balance_available():
    import requests
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    payload = {
        "account_id": "ACC003",
        "linked_account_id": "SAV003",
        "amount": 150,
        "overdraft_token": "valid_token_789"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["status"] == "approved"
    assert data["savings_balance_after"] == 0
    assert data["fee"] == 35
    assert data["checking_balance_after"] == 100

tests.name: test_overdraft_partial_draw_when_savings_partially_insufficient
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance = $100
Linked savings account exists with available balance = $400
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Overdraft fee = $35
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $450 (exceeds checking, within savings + checking)
Verify transaction logic applies fee correctly
trace: R-FIN-012: decision table → checking insufficient AND savings sufficient → approve, debit savings
R-FIN-012: expected outcomes → linked savings debited, fee assessed
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_partial_draw_when_savings_partially_insufficient():
    import requests
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    payload = {
        "account_id": "ACC004",
        "linked_account_id": "SAV004",
        "amount": 450,
        "overdraft_token": "valid_token_101"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["status"] == "approved"
    assert data["savings_debited"] == 350
    assert data["savings_balance_after"] == 50
    assert data["fee"] == 35

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_missing_linked_account
preconditions: Customer account exists with checking balance = $200
Overdraft protection flag = enabled
Linked savings account = null or unset
actions: Omit or set linked_account_id to null
tests.executable:
def test_overdraft_declined_missing_linked_account():
    import requests
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    payload = {
        "account_id": "ACC005",
        "linked_account_id": None,
        "amount": 300,
        "overdraft_token": "valid_token_202"
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["error_code"] == "overdraft_misconfigured"
    assert "linked account" in data["message"].lower()
